qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: http_request follows off-allowlist redirects to attacker-chosen public hosts
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an outbound redirect allowlist bypass in src/tools/http_request.rs and src/tools/web.rs, while the advisory is about workspace path validation and filesystem boundary escapes in src/security/path.rs and validate_path_in_workspace callers.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's workspace-boundary root cause. The sole qualifying finding concerns off-allowlist HTTP redirects, not dangling symlink traversal, post-validation TOCTOU on filesystem paths, or hardlink alias bypass in workspace path enforcement.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes three workspace path-boundary risks: dangling symlink component bypass, validate/use TOCTOU, and hardlink alias bypass, centered on src/security/path.rs and tools that call validate_path_in_workspace before file I/O.
The only qualifying finding's code locations are src/tools/http_request.rs and src/tools/web.rs, and its exploit path is an HTTP redirect chain that bypasses allowed_domains for outbound requests.

The session artifact artifacts/component_family_checklist.md explicitly records the filesystem/path family as pruned, noting workspace tools were fenced by validate_path_in_workspace, TOCTOU revalidation, and hardlink write checks.
